old_claim.note: Genetic Engineering is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim.note: Genetic engineering provides a major construction toolkit and predecessor context for synthetic biology, but broad synthetic biology also includes DNA synthesis, systems modeling, standardized parts, circuits, pathways, and cell-free designs.

source_shape.source_locator: Key Points and abstract describing synthetic biology as assembly of genetic parts, biological building blocks, circuits, pathways, and engineered organisms, including its supplementation of traditional genetic and metabolic engineering.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review describes synthetic biology as construction of biological components, networks, pathways, and engineered organisms, and says it supplements traditional genetic and metabolic engineering efforts.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports an enabling field relationship because genetic engineering is a major toolkit and predecessor context, while the review frames synthetic biology as a broader design-and-assembly discipline rather than a single hard method.

ontology_before: The broad Synthetic Biology node treated the broad Genetic Engineering field as a hard prerequisite across the whole synthetic-biology scope.
ontology_after: The edge now records genetic engineering as an enabling toolkit and predecessor context, while the Synthetic Biology node remains broader than direct organism-genome manipulation.

demotion_preserves.reason: DNA synthesis and oligonucleotide construction remain explicit enabling capabilities for building designed genetic parts, circuits, and pathways.
Systems biology remains explicit model-and-network context for synthetic-biology design across circuits, pathways, and cellular programs.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed deliberately: broad synthetic biology no longer has a single hard broad-field prerequisite, while construction and modeling context remains explicit.
would_reject_if: A source showed that every in-scope realization of synthetic biology requires direct organism-genome manipulation as modeled by the Genetic Engineering node.
The Synthetic Biology node were narrowed to engineered genome modification only, rather than parts, circuits, pathways, and broader design-and-assembly work.
The correction removed the DNA synthesis or systems-biology context edges that preserve core synthetic-biology construction and modeling capabilities.
short_reason: Genetic engineering enables and precedes much synthetic biology, but the broad synthetic-biology field should not hard-depend on one broad field node.
